text: The Locusts, Fire, and Plumb Line

segments.text: "This is what the Lord GOD showed me: He was preparing swarms of locusts just after the king’s harvest, as the late spring crop was coming up.[^26]
"And when the locusts had eaten every green plant in the land, I said,
Lord GOD, please forgive! How will Jacob survive, since he is so small?
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: "So the LORD relented from this plan.
It will not happen,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said.
"This is what the Lord GOD showed me: The Lord GOD was calling for judgment by fire. It consumed the great deep and devoured the land.
"Then I said,
Lord GOD, please stop! How will Jacob survive, since he is so small?
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: "So the LORD relented from this plan.
It will not happen either,
segments.speaker: the Lord God

segments.text: said the Lord GOD.
"This is what He showed me: Behold, the Lord was standing by a wall true to plumb, with a plumb line in His hand.
Amos, what do you see?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: asked the LORD.
A plumb line,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I replied.
Behold,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: said the Lord,
I am setting a plumb line among My people Israel; I will no longer spare them:
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The high places of Isaac will be deserted, and the sanctuaries of Israel will be laid waste; and I will rise up against the house of Jeroboamwith My sword.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: "Then Amaziah the priest of Bethel sent word to Jeroboam king of Israel, saying,
Amos has conspired against you in the midst of the house of Israel. The land cannot bear all his words,
segments.speaker: the Lord
the Lord

segments.text: "And Amaziah said to Amos,
Go away, you seer! Flee to the land of Judah; earn your bread there and do your prophesying there.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But never prophesy at Bethel again, because it is the sanctuary of the king and the temple of the kingdom.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I was not[^27] a prophet,
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: Amos replied,
nor was I the son[^28] of a prophet; rather, I was a herdsman and a tender of sycamore-fig trees.
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text: But the LORD took me from following the flock and said to me, ‘Go, prophesy to My people Israel.’
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text:
Now, therefore, hear the word of the LORD. You say:
‘Do not prophesy against Israel; do not preach against the house of Isaac.’
segments.speaker: Amos

segments.text:
Therefore this is what the LORD says:
‘Your wife will become a prostitute in the city, and your sons and daughters will fall by the sword. Your land will be divided by a measuring line, and you yourself will die on pagan[^29] soil. And Israel will surely go into exile, away from their homeland.’
segments.speaker: Amos
